compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, I can invoke /dso:brainstorm on any ticket type and choose how to proceed
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a type-check gate at brainstorm entry that detects non-epic ticket types and presents convert/enrich options.
**Why**: Foundation for all non-epic brainstorm support — all downstream stories depend on the type detection and routing.
**Scope**:
- IN: Detect ticket type on brainstorm invocation, present convert-to-epic / enrich-in-place choice for non-epics, preserve existing epic behavior unchanged
- OUT: Convert logic (Story 2), enrich logic (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, /dso:brainstorm invoked on a story, task, or bug ticket detects the non-epic type and presents two options: convert to epic or enrich in-place
  ← Satisfies: "brainstorm accepts story, task, and bug ticket IDs in addition to epics"
- When this story is complete, /dso:brainstorm invoked on an epic ticket proceeds through the existing full brainstorm flow with no behavioral change
  ← Satisfies: "When brainstorm is invoked on an epic, existing behavior is unchanged"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Conditional branching by ticket type in SKILL.md — keep the gate minimal and route to clearly separated paths
- [Testing] The existing brainstorm eval suite tests only epic-flow behavior. New eval test cases needed for non-epic detection and option presentation. Existing eval assertions must continue passing.

comments.body: All implementation tasks complete: type detection gate added to brainstorm SKILL.md with RED/GREEN test cycle and eval coverage
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
